dummy/calculator.py.purpose: This file implements a simple console-based calculator application, providing basic arithmetic operations (addition, subtraction, multiplication, division) through user interaction.

key_entities.name: add
key_entities.usage_behavior: Takes two numerical inputs (x, y) and returns their sum.
key_entities.assumptions_context: Inputs are expected to be numerical.

key_entities.name: subtract
key_entities.usage_behavior: Takes two numerical inputs (x, y) and returns their difference (x - y).
key_entities.assumptions_context: Inputs are expected to be numerical.

key_entities.name: multiply
key_entities.usage_behavior: Takes two numerical inputs (x, y) and returns their product.
key_entities.assumptions_context: Inputs are expected to be numerical.

key_entities.name: divide
key_entities.description: Performs division of two numbers, including error handling for division by zero.
key_entities.usage_behavior: Takes two numerical inputs (x, y). Returns the quotient (x / y) or an error string if y is zero.
key_entities.assumptions_context: Inputs are expected to be numerical. The second input (y) is checked to prevent division by zero.

key_entities.name: main
key_entities.description: The main entry point for the calculator application, handling user interface, input parsing, operation selection, and result display.
key_entities.usage_behavior: Executes in a loop, prompting the user for an operation choice (1-4 or 'q') and two numbers. Calls the corresponding arithmetic function and prints the result. Handles invalid input and division by zero messages. Exits when 'q' is entered.
key_entities.assumptions_context: Assumes a console environment for user input and output. Expects numerical input for calculations, handling `ValueError` for non-numerical inputs.

side_effects: Prints messages and results to the console (stdout).
Reads user input from the console (stdin).

dummy/hello_world.c.purpose: This C program serves as a minimal example, demonstrating basic program execution and console output, typically used as a 'Hello World' entry point.

key_entities.name: main
key_entities.description: The entry point of the C program, responsible for initiating execution.
key_entities.usage_behavior: Takes no arguments (void) and returns an integer status code (0 for success). It prints the string "Hello World!" to standard output.

side_effects: Prints a string to standard output (console).
